description: A designed comparison-style infographic with a dark, gritty black asphalt/stone texture background, faintly showing a pair of metal handcuffs lying near the bottom center. The headline at top in white sans-serif (with 'law enforcement officers' bolded) reads: 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two contrasting columns separated by a vertical divider. The left column has a red banner labeled 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' with a large white figure '8 to 10' and caption: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' The right column has a blue banner labeled 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' with a large white figure '2' and caption: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' Bottom left shows the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' text; bottom right reads 'ICE.gov/detainers'. The messaging technique is a stark numeric contrast (8-10 vs 2) designed to pressure local jurisdictions to honor ICE detainers, framing non-cooperation (sanctuary policies) as dangerous and resource-wasting. Target audience: local law enforcement agencies, policymakers, and the public to build support for detainer cooperation.
notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
